metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/llm-framework.md` (line 60) to the edit set and update 'Only `pyyaml` is a hard runtime dependency' to name all three base deps (pyyaml, jsonschema, referencing).
findings.finding: docs/llm-framework.md:60 carries the same stale 'only pyyaml' claim as the named targets but is absent from the plan's edit set, so it will remain incorrect after the fix is applied.
findings.suggested_fix: Change docs/llm-framework.md:60 from 'Only `pyyaml` is a hard runtime dependency (the workflow DSL loader); everything else is one of these extras.' to 'Only `pyyaml`, `jsonschema`, and `referencing` are hard runtime dependencies; everything else is one of these extras.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for each AC — e.g., `grep -rn 'mcp>='` and `grep -rn 'referencing'` anchored to the relevant files — so that success produces machine-checkable evidence rather than relying on manual re-reading.
findings.finding: No concrete proving command exists for any of the three acceptance criteria; AC3 explicitly defers verification to 'verify by re-reading it,' which is a prose hedge rather than a deterministic command that produces machine-checkable evidence on success.
findings.suggested_fix:
Append a 'Verification' section:
```
# Verify
grep -n 'referencing' README.md docs/config.md   # must appear in both
grep -n 'mcp>=1.9' README.md                       # must show mcp>=1.9
grep -n 'Three runtime' pyproject.toml             # must show updated comment
grep -n 'mcp>=1.9' pyproject.toml                  # ground-truth matches floor
```
Each command must exit 0 (match found) to satisfy its AC.
